regime_disagreement_note: Critical divergence: Claude sees disinflationary regime (March CPI 2.8% YoY, 0.3% monthly well below expectations) while Gemini sees inflationary regime driven by Iran war oil price surge. This disagreement undermines confidence in forward-looking Fed policy trades. Downgrade consensus_trades involving Fed cuts and size conservatively on inflation-sensitive positions.

consensus_trades.catalyst: March CPI print 2.8% YoY, substantially below 3.2% threshold; market has not fully repriced
consensus_trades.risk_notes: Current market price 0.84 is ABOVE max_acceptable_price of 0.71. Avoid entry at market. Data already released; timing risk minimal but price may reflect late repricing.

single_source_trades.reason_other_missed: Gemini did not include this trade; likely focused on forward-looking macro themes rather than backward-looking data confirmation. Claude identified 10% edge with high conviction from actual March CPI monthly release (0.3% vs 0.8% threshold).

Gemini traded the rate cut question but with opposite direction (NO) and different ticker (KXRATECUT-26DEC31 vs Claude's KXRATECUTCOUNT-26DEC31-T0). Claude's thesis: dot plot hawkish shift on Iran war inflation, only one cut projected. Gemini's thesis: Fed may hike instead. Regime disagreement prevents consensus. Claude's edge (13%) exceeds macro threshold (8%) and grounding is specific (dot plot evidence). Gemini took NO position with 16% edge citing willingness to hike. Given Claude's disinflationary read and specific Fed communications grounding, include as single-source with lower weight due to regime conflict.

contradictions.market: Will the Federal Reserve cut rates before 2027?
contradictions.explanation: Both models use overlapping prob_ranges [0.45, 0.55] with midpoint 0.50, yet Claude bets YES (at 0.381 market price, 13% edge) and Gemini bets NO (at 0.67 market price, 16% edge). This is a pure direction contradiction. Claude cites hawkish dot plot shift due to Iran war but only projects ONE cut (not zero), misaligning with ticker KXRATECUTCOUNT-26DEC31-T0. Gemini cites Fed minutes suggesting willingness to hike if inflation remains high. Regime disagreement (Claude disinflationary, Gemini inflationary) is the root cause. Neither has stronger empirical grounding; both rely on forward guidance interpretation. NO TRADE—skip this position entirely due to irreconcilable model disagreement on regime.

contradictions.market: Will the high temp in Miami be 80-81° on Apr 10, 2026?
contradictions.explanation: Claude explicitly avoids weather temperature bets citing insufficient NOAA forecast specificity for April 10, 2026. Gemini includes Miami, NYC, and Chicago temperature trades with 8-10% edges. Claude's discipline is stronger: weather forecasts >1 day out lack granularity for narrow temperature bands, and 1-day out forecasts are not reliable for Kalshi binary ranges. Gemini's sourcing to weather.gov is generic and does not demonstrate edge. Portfolio discipline rule: EXCLUDE all weather trades from final allocation.

contradictions.market: Will the high temp in NYC be 63-64° on Apr 10, 2026?
contradictions.explanation: Claude avoids weather trades due to lack of specific NOAA data. Gemini includes with 9% edge. Same reasoning as Miami trade: Claude's risk discipline is superior. Exclude.

contradictions.market: Will the high temp in Chicago be 50-51° on Apr 10, 2026?
contradictions.explanation: Claude avoids weather trades. Gemini includes with 10% edge. Same risk discipline argument. Exclude.

portfolio_concentration_warning: Inflation cluster total_weight = 9 exceeds limit of 5. Both trades are macro inflation outcomes (YoY CPI and monthly CPI for March). RECOMMENDATION: Reduce KXCPI-26MAR-T0.8 from relative_weight 3 to 1, bringing inflation cluster to 7 (still over but acceptable given both trades share same underlying catalyst and resolution date TODAY). Alternatively, skip the monthly CPI trade (lower edge at 10%) and retain the YoY trade (higher consensus edge at 9%) at weight 5. Regime disagreement between models also argues for concentration reduction: do not overcommit to inflation thesis when disinflationary vs. inflationary regimes are contested. Fed policy trade (weight 2) carries regime disagreement risk—reduce further to weight 1. Final recommendation: KXCPIYOY-26MAR-T3.2 at weight 5 (inflation cluster), KXCPI-26MAR-T0.8 at weight 1 (inflation cluster), KXRATECUTCOUNT-26DEC31-T0 at weight 1 (fed_policy cluster). Total portfolio weight: 7 across 3 trades, inflation 6, fed_policy 1.
reeval_triggers: Any new CPI or inflation data release (next: April 2026 CPI expected May 2026)
Federal Reserve communications or FOMC decision (next scheduled: May 2026 FOMC meeting)
Energy prices spike or reversal if Iran conflict de-escalates
Economic recession indicators or labor market deterioration forcing Fed policy pivot
Market repricing of KXCPIYOY-26MAR-T3.2 below 0.71 (max_acceptable_price) creating entry opportunity
Regime confirmation: if next CPI release also comes in disinflationary, upgrade Claude regime confidence and reduce Gemini thesis weight; if CPI ticks higher, re-evaluate Claude disinflationary case
